patterns.comments: Here we need the \w modifier in order to check that the words are allowed
Prevent captures of `|>` as a keyword when defining custom operator like `<|>`
Capture // when inside of (* *) like that the rule which capture comments starting by // is not trigger. See https://github.com/ionide/ionide-fsgrammar/issues/155
patterns.name: fast-capture.comment.line.double-slash.clef

patterns.comments: Capture (*) when inside of (* *) so that it doesn't prematurely end the comment block.
patterns.name: fast-capture.comment.line.mul-operator.clef

patterns.comments: Here we need the \w modifier in order to check that the words isn't blacklisted

patterns.comment: Because we first capture the keywords, we can capture what looks like a word and assume it's an entity definition

patterns.beginComment: The group (?![[:space:]]*\) is for protection against overload operator. static member (<)
patterns.endComment: The group (?<!:) prevent us from stopping on :> when using SRTP synthax

patterns.name: binding.clef
patterns.begin: \b(let mutable|static let mutable|static let|let inline|let|and inline|and|member inline|static member inline|static member|member|let!)(\s+rec|mutable)?(\s+\[\<.*\>\])?\s*(private|internal|public)?\s+(\[[^-=]*\]|[_[:alpha:]]([_[:alpha:]0-9\._]+)*|``[_[:alpha:]]([_[:alpha:]0-9\._`\s]+|(?<=,)\s)*)?

patterns.comments: Here we need the \w modifier in order to check that the words isn't blacklisted
